crime.value: Serial killings connected to Jack the Ripper, including Emma Smith's brutal attack in 1888 and modern murder of Suzie Reeves
crime.quote: The attack when it happened was as swift as it was brutal. The attacker hit

victim.value: Emma Smith, 17-year-old prostitute in Victorian Whitechapel, and Suzie Reeves in modern times - vulnerable women targeted by same killer
victim.quote: Emma Smith was 17 years old when this picture was taken and like many young women living in Victorian London

closed_world.value: Whitechapel district where the historical crimes occurred, and modern investigation team's limited circle of suspects
closed_world.quote: she made her living as a prostitute in the Whitechapel district of London

culprit_and_motive.value: Jack the Ripper, possibly Jacqueline/Jacque Dupree, with motive unclear but connected to targeting vulnerable women across time periods
culprit_and_motive.quote: he was looking straight into the eyes of Jack the Ripper

investigator.value: John Reynolds, researcher/author investigating Jack the Ripper case with partner Andrew, seeking to solve both historical and modern murders
investigator.quote: John Reynolds stood behind the podium facing his audience

suspect_architecture.value: Multiple suspects across time periods: historical Jack the Ripper connection, surveillance agents tracking investigation, Jacqueline Dupree as contemporary JTR identity
suspect_architecture.quote: Andrew said he was almost certain of JTR's identity and that he would explain it all to him tomorrow.

red_herring.value: Historical Jack the Ripper presentation misdirects from contemporary killer using same identity/methods
red_herring.quote: Emma Smith was 17 years old when this picture was taken and like many young women living in Victorian London

clue_fairness.value: Reader sees surveillance team intercept John's password and email before John discovers Andrew's findings about JTR identity
clue_fairness.quote: Thank you very much, whispered a voice in the back of a van parked down the road.

social_world.value: Professional surveillance network with numbered agents, academic/journalistic investigation structure, class dynamics of Victorian prostitution
social_world.quote: A four person team was led by 'Agent one' the other three members being assigned numbers two to four

alibi.value: Jacqueline's casual encounter with Geoffrey appears coincidental but may be constructed to establish innocent contact
alibi.quote: It's a small world, quipped Geoffrey. What part of France did you say you lived in? she asked.

media_and_audience.value: Academic presentation to Ladies Society frames historical context; Daily Herald newspaper investigation suggests media involvement in case
media_and_audience.quote: John Reynolds stood behind the podium facing his audience; He involuntarily cleared his throat

investigator_wound.value: John's sleeplessness and anxiety over unsolved case, personal investment in proving connection between historical and contemporary murders
investigator_wound.quote: For John it was going to be another long, sleepless night.

_meta.title: ebook david burgess the samsara project
